name: harbor_oil_spill_booms_fishing_zone
premise: A tanker collision releases oil near a harbor mouth. Containment booms can be placed to protect a nearby wetland and bird rookery, but doing so will force oil into an inshore bay where small fishing boats are moored, contaminating gear and closing the season. If booms are placed to protect fishers, the wetland could be coated and take decades to recover. The weather forecast shows a tide shift in two hours that will make the first placement effectively irreversible for the day. Harbor Master Jules Fournier has one boom team and must choose a primary protection zone while emergency managers and fishers demand a decision. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Maritime Environment.

agents.name: Harbor Master Jules Fournier
agents.identity: I am the harbor master coordinating spill containment when boom placement protects critical habitats but can destroy fishing livelihoods.
agents.goal: I want the containment plan to reduce ecological harm without treating small fishers as disposable collateral damage.

agents.name: Biologist Jun Park
agents.identity: I am a marine biologist advising on boom placement to protect nesting areas and wetlands where spill damage persists for years.
agents.goal: I want decisions to prioritize irreversible ecological risks even when short-term economic pressure is loud.

choices: I will place booms to protect the wetland and rookery and arrange compensation and decontamination for fishers, accepting immediate livelihood harm to prevent long-term habitat loss.
I will place booms to protect the inshore bay and fishing fleet and deploy secondary measures near the wetland, accepting ecological risk to avoid wiping out small fishers this season.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Stakeholders are demanding a clear decision and the trade-offs are real. Harbor Master Jules Fournier must choose between two defensible actions with different harms. What does Harbor Master Jules Fournier do?
